summary: Comprehensive testing of 5 ACA Compliance features: Dashboard MEC calculation, Risk Alerts penalty breakdown, Plan Library MV/MEC logic. All 15 backend tests passed. All frontend UI tests passed.
passed_tests: Backend: Dashboard returns mec_coverage_pct field
Backend: MEC coverage NOT 100% when Platinum PPO has mec_qualified=false (returns 6.4%)
Backend: mec_offered count excludes MEC-failing plans (4 of 5 medical plans)
Backend: Risk alerts include penalty_a_amount, penalty_b_amount, penalty_a_reason, penalty_b_reason
Backend: Penalty A calculated correctly when MEC < 95% ($50,490 = 17 FT * $2,970)
Backend: potential_penalty equals penalty_a_amount + penalty_b_amount
Backend: Platinum PPO has mec_qualified=false
Backend: Bronze HDHP has mv_certified=true (MV 58% but certified)
Backend: Silver PPO has mec_qualified=true
Backend: Dental/vision plans have mec_qualified=false (expected for non-medical)
Backend: MV failing plans identified correctly
Backend: All medical plans have mv_percentage and mv_certified fields
Backend: Compliance check returns MEC data
Backend: Compliance check returns MV data
Backend: Compliance check for Platinum PPO shows MEC not qualified
Frontend: Dashboard shows MEC Coverage 6.4% (not 100%)
Frontend: Dashboard shows Potential Penalty $50,490
Frontend: Dashboard shows penalty breakdown with 4980H(a) reason
Frontend: Plan Library shows MEC Fail badge on Platinum PPO (medical, mec_qualified=false)
Frontend: Plan Library does NOT show MEC Fail badge on dental/vision plans
Frontend: Bronze HDHP shows MV 58% badge (blue, not amber Fail) - certified
Frontend: Bronze HDHP Assign button is enabled (mv_certified=true)
Frontend: TEST MV Fail Plan shows MV 55% (Fail) badge (amber)
Frontend: TEST MV Fail Plan Assign button is disabled (mv_certified=false)
Frontend: TEST MV Fail Plan shows Get Actuarial Quote button
Frontend: Edit Platinum PPO - MEC checkbox is disabled with '(Failed — locked)' text
Frontend: Edit Silver PPO - MEC checkbox is enabled
seed_data_creation: Created temporary TEST MV Fail Plan (MV 55%, mv_certified=false) to test Get Actuarial Quote button and disabled Assign button. Cleaned up after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 ACA Compliance features tested and working: 1) Get Actuarial Quote button shows ONLY when MV < 60% AND mv_certified=false, 2) Assign button disabled when MV fails (MV < 60% AND not certified), 3) MEC checkbox disabled in edit popup when mec_qualified=false with '(Failed — locked)' text, 4) Dashboard risk alerts show $50,490 penalty with 4980H(a) breakdown, 5) Dashboard MEC coverage % uses plan_library collection (shows 6.4%, not 100%). Key data: Platinum PPO (mec_qualified=false) shows MEC Fail badge, Bronze HDHP (MV 58%, mv_certified=true) does NOT show MV Fail or Get Quote button. Dental/vision plans do NOT show MEC Fail badges.

dashboard.penalty_breakdown: $50,490 — 4980H(a): MEC not offered to 95%+ of FT employees

plan_library.platinum_ppo: Shows MEC Fail badge (red), Assign enabled (mv_certified=true)
plan_library.bronze_hdhp: Shows MV 58% badge (blue), MEC badge, Assign enabled (mv_certified=true)
plan_library.test_mv_fail_plan: Shows MV 55% (Fail) badge (amber), Assign disabled, Get Actuarial Quote button visible
plan_library.dental_vision: No MEC Fail badges (correct - only medical plans show MEC status)

edit_popup.platinum_ppo: MEC checkbox disabled with '(Failed — locked)' text
